title: Deezer
author: a4zv
body:
## Description
<!-- A clear and concise description of what the bug is. -->

## To Reproduce
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to '...'
2. Click on '....'
3. Scroll down to '....'
4. See error

## Expected behavior
When playing a Deezer track through LavaSrc, the track should decrypt correctly and play normally without any audio corruption or playback exceptions. Track loading and metadata resolution succeed, but playback fails. Without the `formats` option configured, the track plays with heavily distorted audio and repeated `mpg123` decoder errors (`Frankenstein stream`, `bad frame`, `unable to get valid sideinfo`). When specifying `formats` (tested with `MP3_128`, `MP3_320`, and both together), playback fails with `java.lang.IllegalStateException: Mp3 frame not found`.

## Version info
**Client used:** 
**Client used:** Wavelink (Python)
**Lavalink version:** 4.2.2
**LavaSrc version:** 4.8.3
**Java version:** 17
**Operating System:** Ubuntu 24.04 (Docker)

title: Cannot start Lavalink without IPv4
author: DRSchlaubi
body:
## Description
Lavalink tries to pull plugins from `maven.lavalink.dev` which is IPv4 only

## To Reproduce
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start Lavalink on an IPv6 only server

## Expected behavior
It works fine

## Additional context
Whilst the best way to fix it would be to add IPv6 support to maven.lavalink.dev, using NAT64 can also help, however by default the plugin downloader tries to use IPv4

title: Does Discord DAVE (E2EE) affect Lavalink?
author: aruo319
body:
Hello Lavalink team,

I have a question regarding Discord's new DAVE (End-to-End Encrypted audio) implementation.

Is Lavalink currently compatible with or affected by Discord's DAVE (E2EE) voice encryption?
If not, are there any plans to support or adapt to DAVE in the future, or is Lavalink expected to continue working independently from Discord's E2EE voice pipeline?

I'm maintaining a Discord bot that uses Lavalink, and I want to make sure there will be no compatibility issues going forward.

Thank you for your work on Lavalink.

title: Persistent Plugin Issues: "No plugin manifest found" with LavaSrc & "Please sign in" with youtube-plugin
author: FDJHBYB
body:
Hello everyone,

I'm setting up a Lavalink server for my Discord bot and have run into a persistent and complex issue that I haven't been able to solve after extensive troubleshooting. I would greatly appreciate any expert insight.

My Environment:

Lavalink Version: 4.1.1
Java Version: 17
OS: Windows Server
The Problem:

I am facing two different issues depending on the YouTube plugin I use:

1. With lavasrc-4.7.0.jar (The Recommended Plugin):

This is the plugin I want to use. However, Lavalink fails to start every single time.

Steps Taken: I create a completely clean directory containing only Lavalink.jar and an application.yml file. I then create a plugins folder and place a freshly downloaded lavasrc-4.7.0.jar inside.
Result: Lavalink crashes on startup.
Error: The root cause is always java.lang.RuntimeException: No plugin manifest found in .\plugins\lavasrc-4.7.0.jar. This error persists even after trying multiple clean directories and re-downloading the JAR file multiple times to ensure it's not corrupted.
2. With youtube-plugin-1.13.3.jar (The Older Plugin):

Following advice to test this, I tried the older plugin.

Partial Success: Surprisingly, with this JAR in the plugins folder, Lavalink starts successfully! It doesn't crash with the "manifest" error. My bot can connect, and it can successfully search for tracks using ytsearch.
The Failure: The problem occurs during playback. When Lavalink tries to play a track, it fails immediately.
Error: The log shows a com.sedmelluq.discord.lavaplayer.tools.FriendlyException: Please sign in.
Attempted Fix: To solve this, I created a new burner Google account, enabled 2FA, and generated a 16-digit App Password. I configured my application.yml with the email and the App Password, but the Please sign in error still occurs.
Summary of My Dilemma:

I am stuck in a difficult situation:

The recommended modern plugin (LavaSrc) fails to load entirely on my system for an unknown environmental reason.
The old plugin (youtube-plugin) loads successfully but fails during playback due to the Please sign in error, and its email/password authentication method doesn't seem to work.
My Questions for the Community:

What could be causing the persistent No plugin manifest found error with LavaSrc on a Windows Server environment, even when the setup is perfectly clean? Are there known issues with file permissions or antivirus software?
Is the email/password authentication method for the old youtube-plugin now deprecated by Google, making the Please sign in error unfixable with that plugin?
I am available for a screenshare session if an expert has time to look at my setup directly, as I suspect the root cause is an environmental issue.

Thank you for your time and any help you can provide.

title: Periodic Lavalink & Plugin Update Checks with events
author: topi314
body:
### Change request

We should do periodic Lavalink & Lavalink Plugins update checks and notify the clients via events

### Relevance

This should hopefully reduce the issues reported by outdated lavalink or plugin versions

### Could this be a plugin instead?

hell nah

### Checklist

- [x] I have checked for duplicate issues
- [x] I have checked for existing plugins

title: com.sedmelluq.discord.lavaplayer.tools.FriendlyException: Something went wrong while looking up the track.
author: JimMarley420
body:
## Description

When I try to play a YouTube playlist using my bot connected to my Lavalink nodes running on an Ubuntu VPS, the bot fails to load the tracks from the playlist.

## To Reproduce
Connect my bot to Lavalink nodes on an Ubuntu VPS.

Use the command:
/play https://youtube.com/playlist?list=RDEMivmOjajBbYvKJvQj4BrVdw&playnext=1&si=VIgwAotr6stncMm1

Observe the error in Lavalink logs and the failure to play the music.

## Expected behavior
play the music

```

```

title: Decoding specific loadtracks encoded base64 on decodetrack endpoint throw an error
author: Deivu
body:
## Description
Doing a `/loadtracks` request on specific links, grabbing the encoded value, then decoding it through `/decodetrack `endpoint can throw `Status Code 500 (Internal Server Error)`

## To Reproduce
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Do a request in `/loadtracks` endpoint with identifier query set to `https://youtu.be/uPX5zQfDxxE?list=RDihaE_4246ig`

2. Get the `data.encoded` value

3. Do a request in `/decodetrack` with the track query set to the value you got in step 2

4. See error

## Expected behavior

Should not throw an error since its  a lavalink encoded track, not from any third party libs

## Version info
**Client used:** Shoukaku

Output of `java -version`:
```
// Using your docker image, checking at env variables, the java ver is:
ENV JAVA_VERSION=jdk-18.0.2.1+1
```
